note: Conclusion that ACD+ACB sum > ABC+BCA sum should follow from CN.2 (adding ACB to the inequality from arg_i=3) and CN.1 (transitivity). Prop.13 does not justify the inequality itself.
CN.3 (subtract equals) is extraneous. The step only requires Prop.13 to establish that ACD+ACB = 2 right angles (a straight angle). No subtraction is performed here.

Similarly-we-can-show step should mirror the proof of arg_i=2-6. The analogous cases require Prop.16 (exterior angle), Prop.13 (straight angle sum), and CN.2 (adding angles). Prop.16 alone is insufficient.
To conclude ABD > ACB requires transitivity: ABD = ADB (from arg_i=4, Prop.5) and ADB > ACB (from arg_i=3, Prop.16). CN.1 (transitivity) is needed before CN.5 applies.

To show BA+AC > BC requires: DB > BC (arg_i=6, Prop.19) and DB = BA+AC (by construction, arg_i=3). CN.1 (transitivity) must chain these. CN.2 (adding equals) does not apply here; the inequality is not created by addition.
Similarly-we-can-show step should mirror args 3-8 structure. Analogous cases require construction (Post.2, Prop.3), isosceles reasoning (Prop.5), and transitivity (CN.1), not just Prop.19.
Step concludes BDC much greater than BAC. This requires transitivity: BDC > CEB (arg_i=10) and CEB > BAC (arg_i=11) implies BDC > BAC via CN.1, not CN.5 (whole > part).
Step: FD=FK (by Def.15) and FD=A (construction) implies FK=A. This is CN.1 (transitivity), not Def.16. Def.16 only names the center; the radius equality comes from Def.15.
Prop.5 gives DGF = DFG (isosceles). To show DFG > EGF requires: DGF > EGF (from prior reasoning) and DFG = DGF. CN.1 (transitivity) chains these before CN.5 applies.
Step concludes EFG much greater than EGF. EFG = DFG + angle CEG, and DFG > EGF, so EFG > EGF by CN.5 (whole > part), not Prop.19 (which relates side lengths to angles).
